title: Daily Briefing — Jun 11
headline: Volatility’s back, tech’s stumbling, and fear is finally showing up in the crowd.
body: Right, so let’s look at what the flows are telling us… The market’s flashing elevated volatility with VIX at 22.22 (well above its 20-day at 17.46), SPY grinding up slightly to $728.32 but still below its 20 MA ($744.92)—that’s a textbook mean reversion warning. Fear & Greed is stuck at 27.2, proper 'Fear' territory, and a full reversal from the 'Greed' we saw a month ago.

Sector rotation is starting to pick up: XLK (Tech) is weakening despite relative strength, while XLF (Financials) and XLV (Healthcare) are improving, but at 'elevated' risk—so don’t go piling in. Utilities (XLU) are lagging, and Energy (XLE) flagged 'HIGH' risk, so avoid both. Spotting defensive improvement in XLV, XLP, and XLF, but nothing screams 'safe haven' yet.

Contrarian signals are blossoming: Fear & Greed this low (<25 would be a buy per the rule, but 27 is close), put/call is not extreme, and smart money hasn’t flipped bullish yet. Most tech heavyweights—AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META—are all well below their daily 20 MAs, triggering the 'exit long positions' rule. If bonds freak out (which they aren’t—HYG, LQD, TLT all steady and above their 20s), everything breaks, but for now, patience trumps prediction. Retail isn’t panic-selling, but the crowd’s certainly nervous.
callout_body:
SPY: $744.92 (20 MA), watch for rejection or reclaim
QQQ: $720.67 (20 MA), currently $702.09—bear bias continue
XLK: $183.55 (20 MA), $178.77 and falling
XLF: $52.15, just above 20 MA $51.69—triggered bullish but stay cautious
HYG, LQD, TLT: Above 20 MA, risk-off not triggered
Fear & Greed: 27.2—near extreme fear zone, not quite there
VIX: 22.22 (Elevated), volatility regime in force
action_items: Exit or reduce longs in tech names below daily 20 MA (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META)
Watch XLV, XLF, XLP for defensive sector rotation but wait for confirmation—don’t chase
closing_quote: If it’s in the press, it’s in the price — less trades = more wins.
